title: Copy-up with tmpfs apparently breaks with symlinks
body:
### Issue Description

Mounting a tmpfs on `/run` has some strange effects with gitlab image.
The image has `/run/motd.dynamic` symlinked to `/dev/null`, which seems to break the copy-up

Disabling copy-up works around the issue:

`/usr/bin/podman run --rm --mount=type=tmpfs,destination=/run,notmpcopyup docker.io/gitlab/gitlab-ce:16.4.0-ce.0`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman run --rm --tmpfs /run:rw docker.io/gitlab/gitlab-ce:16.4.0-ce.0`
    or: `podman run --rm --systemd=always docker.io/gitlab/gitlab-ce:16.4.0-ce.0`

### Describe the results you received

Fails to start with

``` bash
Error: OCI runtime error: crun: chmod `run/motd.dynamic`: Operation not supported
```

### Describe the results you expected

Well.. the container should run

comments.body:
This is a new kernel issue, that I believe is fixed in podman 4.7.1 and the latest crun.
Basically the kernel used to allow users to chmod on symlinks and ignore it, now it returns an error.

> crun-1.9.2-1

Means its fixed already? Thanks
I did not change Kernel Version (still 6.1), but I updated debian to 12.2, means they probably backported the change.
Yes the latest code will  no longer attempt to chmod on symlinks.
